topicGroups.title: Enterprise Behaviours - Initiative
topicGroups.introSummary: This strand focuses on acting early, improving work, helping others, and stepping up before being told.
topicGroups.writePrompt: Write one or two EST-ready sentences explaining how initiative can be shown in a workplace situation.
topicGroups.sampleResponse: Initiative can be shown when a worker acts proactively, suggests improvements, helps colleagues, or volunteers for extra responsibilities before being told. This matters because it improves productivity and shows the worker can contribute positively to the workplace.

topicGroups.title: Time Management Skills - Plan and prioritise tasks to meet deadlines
topicGroups.introSummary: This strand is about ordering tasks clearly, using tools well, and adjusting when deadlines shift.
topicGroups.writePrompt: Write one or two EST-ready sentences explaining how a student or worker can plan and prioritise tasks to meet deadlines.
topicGroups.sampleResponse: Time management involves planning ahead, prioritising urgent tasks, and using tools such as calendars, lists, or reminders to stay organised. This helps a person meet deadlines because responsibilities are visible, manageable, and easier to adjust when circumstances change.

topicGroups.title: Managing personal finance, budgeting, seeking assistance, unexpected financial events inc changes to financial circumstance
topicGroups.introSummary: This strand trains budgeting, seeking support, and responding to financial changes without panic.
topicGroups.writePrompt: Write one or two EST-ready sentences explaining how budgeting and seeking assistance support personal financial management.
topicGroups.sampleResponse: Budgeting helps a person balance income and expenses, identify unnecessary spending, and plan for unexpected events. Seeking assistance from trusted services or experts also supports financial management because it provides reliable advice and helps people make informed decisions.

topicGroups.title: Cover Letters, STAR and Addressing Selection Criteria
topicGroups.introSummary: This strand focuses on cover letters, STAR responses, and evidence-rich answers to selection criteria.
topicGroups.writePrompt: Write one or two EST-ready sentences explaining how STAR helps an applicant address selection criteria effectively.
topicGroups.sampleResponse: The STAR method helps applicants address selection criteria by structuring examples into Situation, Task, Action, and Result. This makes a response clearer because the employer can see exactly what the applicant did and what outcome was achieved.

topicGroups.title: Communication Skills
topicGroups.introSummary: This strand focuses on clear messages, active listening, and communication that suits the audience and situation.
topicGroups.writePrompt: Write one or two EST-ready sentences explaining how communication skills can be applied in a workplace or interview situation.
topicGroups.sampleResponse: Communication skills can be applied by using clear verbal language, active listening, and appropriate non-verbal communication for the audience and purpose. This is important because it reduces misunderstandings, builds rapport, and helps tasks or interviews run more effectively.

topicGroups.title: Megatrends, Growth Industries and Labour Market Information
topicGroups.introSummary: This strand focuses on megatrends, growth industries, and labour market evidence that shapes future career opportunities.
topicGroups.writePrompt: Write one or two EST-ready sentences explaining how megatrends, growth industries, or labour market information can influence future career opportunities.
topicGroups.sampleResponse: Megatrends shape the future of work by changing industries, jobs, and skill needs over time. Labour market information and knowledge of growth industries help people make better career decisions because they show where demand is increasing, what qualifications may be needed, and which opportunities are emerging.

initiative.title: Initiative Reactor
initiative.subtitle: Spot the move, name the type, fix the workplace, then upgrade the EST answer.
initiative.memoryHook: PIVHS: Proactive, Improve work practices, Vocalise opinions, Help fellow workers, Seek more responsibilities

steps.title: Step 1: Spot the Initiative
steps.instruction: Decide whether each workplace move shows strong initiative.

items.prompt: A worker notices stock is running low and restocks before customers complain.
options: Shows initiative
Needs more initiative
Only follows instructions
items.correct: Shows initiative
items.feedback: This is proactive. The worker spots a problem early and acts without being told.

items.prompt: A worker sees a spill but leaves it until a supervisor gives exact instructions.
options: Shows initiative
Needs more initiative
Strong teamwork
items.correct: Needs more initiative
items.feedback: This is passive, not initiative. Waiting creates risk and delays.

items.prompt: A worker fixes a messy display before customers start avoiding the area.
options: Shows initiative
Needs more initiative
Just luck
items.correct: Shows initiative
items.feedback: This is initiative because the worker acts before the issue affects customers.

steps.title: Step 2: Name the Initiative Type
steps.instruction: Match each workplace action to the correct type of initiative.

items.prompt: Restocking shelves before products run out.
options: Being proactive
Vocalising opinions
Seeking more responsibilities
items.correct: Being proactive
items.feedback: This is proactive because the worker anticipates a need before it becomes a problem.

items.prompt: Telling a supervisor that clearer shelf signs would help customers find items faster.
options: Helping fellow workers
Vocalising opinions
Being proactive
items.correct: Vocalising opinions
items.feedback: This is vocalising opinions because the worker shares an improvement idea constructively.

items.prompt: Helping a new team member complete a task safely during a busy shift.
options: Helping fellow workers
Improving work practices
Seeking more responsibilities
items.correct: Helping fellow workers
items.feedback: This is helping fellow workers because the action supports teamwork and productivity.

items.prompt: Volunteering to learn the new e-register system.
options: Being proactive
Seeking more responsibilities
Vocalising opinions
items.correct: Seeking more responsibilities
items.feedback: This shows initiative through willingness to take on more and develop new skills.

steps.title: Step 3: Fix the Workplace
steps.instruction: Choose the strongest initiative response for each workplace problem.

items.prompt: Packing tools are often missing, which wastes time for the team. What is the strongest initiative move for a new worker?
options: Suggest setting up supply stations at each bench so tools are always nearby.
Keep searching quietly because improving systems is not a new worker's job.
Wait until a manager gets frustrated enough to notice the problem.
items.correct: Suggest setting up supply stations at each bench so tools are always nearby.
items.feedback: Strong move. This improves work practices and shows even a new worker can contribute useful ideas.

items.prompt: A junior supervisor notices customer queues are longest on Thursday evenings. What is the strongest initiative move?
options: Propose moving more staff into peak periods based on sales patterns.
Leave the roster unchanged because busy times are just part of retail.
Tell workers to move faster without changing staffing levels.
items.correct: Propose moving more staff into peak periods based on sales patterns.
items.feedback: Strong move. This shows initiative at supervisor level by improving systems before service drops.

steps.title: Step 4: Upgrade the Answer
steps.instruction: Choose the sentence that sounds most like a stronger EST answer.

items.prompt: Which sentence best explains initiative in an EST-style answer?
options: I showed initiative by suggesting a safer system for restocking, which improved efficiency and helped the team work more effectively.
I did my job and everything was fine.
Initiative is when things happen at work.
items.correct: I showed initiative by suggesting a safer system for restocking, which improved efficiency and helped the team work more effectively.
items.feedback: This is strongest because it names the action, shows initiative clearly, and explains the workplace effect.

items.prompt: Which sentence best links initiative with teamwork?
options: Initiative can support teamwork when a worker notices a colleague struggling and steps in to help without being told.
Initiative means working alone and not needing anyone else.
Teamwork and initiative are unrelated.
items.correct: Initiative can support teamwork when a worker notices a colleague struggling and steps in to help without being told.
items.feedback: This is strongest because it connects initiative to a real workplace example and explains how the team benefits.

time-management.title: Time Management Control Room
time-management.subtitle: Triage the task list, build the best order, rescue the deadline, then upgrade the EST answer.

steps.title: Step 1: Triage the Task List
steps.instruction: Choose which task should move first because the deadline pressure is highest.

items.prompt: An apprentice plumber has four jobs today. Which task should be the immediate priority?
options: Urgent repair on a burst pipe
Paperwork and logbook
Tidying the vehicle first
items.correct: Urgent repair on a burst pipe
items.feedback: Urgent repair comes first because it has the highest immediate consequence and deadline pressure.

items.prompt: A student has a test tomorrow, a shift on Saturday, and a message about a party tonight. What should be prioritised first?
options: Study for the test and lock in the shift time
Answer the party message first
Do whichever task feels easiest
items.correct: Study for the test and lock in the shift time
items.feedback: Strong time management starts with urgent commitments, not the least demanding task.

items.prompt: Before leaving for the first job, what is the strongest planning move?
options: Check the schedule, tools, materials, and travel route
Start driving and hope everything needed is already in the ute
Ignore the schedule because the supervisor will fix problems later
items.correct: Check the schedule, tools, materials, and travel route
items.feedback: Planning before the task begins reduces delays and makes the whole day easier to manage.

steps.title: Step 2: Build the Best Order
steps.instruction: Choose the sequence that shows the strongest planning process.

items.prompt: Which sequence shows the strongest start-of-day time management process?
options: Review the schedule, prepare tools/materials, travel to the urgent job, then complete lower-priority tasks later
Travel first, check the schedule after arriving, then work out what tools are needed
Start the easiest task, then decide whether the urgent job still matters
items.correct: Review the schedule, prepare tools/materials, travel to the urgent job, then complete lower-priority tasks later
items.feedback: This is strongest because it plans ahead, prepares resources, and sequences urgent work before less urgent tasks.

items.prompt: Which move best shows efficient task sequencing?
options: Pick up supplies on the way to the next job instead of making a separate trip later
Drive back across town twice because it feels more familiar
Leave all materials collection until the end of the day
items.correct: Pick up supplies on the way to the next job instead of making a separate trip later
items.feedback: Grouping related tasks saves time and keeps the day moving more efficiently.

items.prompt: Which tool use best supports sequencing and deadline control?
options: Using a planner, checklist, or calendar to map tasks and times clearly
Keeping all deadlines in your head so planning stays flexible
Waiting until something is overdue before writing it down
items.correct: Using a planner, checklist, or calendar to map tasks and times clearly
items.feedback: Tools matter because they make the sequence visible and easier to track.

steps.title: Step 3: Rescue the Deadline
steps.instruction: Choose the strongest response when the original plan starts to break down.

items.prompt: A job is taking longer than expected and will push the rest of the schedule back. What is the strongest next move?
options: Inform the supervisor, adjust the schedule, and contact the next client about the delay
Say nothing and hope the next job somehow still fits
Abandon the current job without telling anyone
items.correct: Inform the supervisor, adjust the schedule, and contact the next client about the delay
items.feedback: Strong time management includes monitoring progress and adjusting early when delays occur.

items.prompt: An emergency task comes in after the day has already been planned. What shows the strongest time management?
options: Reprioritise the day so the emergency is handled and lower-priority tasks shift accordingly
Ignore the emergency because the original list was already written
Keep all tasks in the same order even if deadlines are now unrealistic
items.correct: Reprioritise the day so the emergency is handled and lower-priority tasks shift accordingly
items.feedback: Good time management is not rigid. It adjusts when workplace conditions change.

items.prompt: A Year 12 student is struggling to balance school, work, sport, and family commitments. What is the strongest advice?
options: Write commitments into a planner, review priorities weekly, and allow buffer time when things change
Take on every commitment and hope it works out
Avoid planning because plans make life feel too controlled
items.correct: Write commitments into a planner, review priorities weekly, and allow buffer time when things change
items.feedback: This is strongest because it combines tools, review habits, and flexibility.

steps.title: Step 4: Upgrade the Answer
steps.instruction: Choose the sentence that sounds most like a stronger EST answer.

items.prompt: Which sentence best explains time management in an EST-style answer?
options: Time management involves planning tasks, prioritising urgent work, and adjusting schedules so deadlines can still be met.
Time management means being busy all the time.
Time management is when work happens eventually.
items.correct: Time management involves planning tasks, prioritising urgent work, and adjusting schedules so deadlines can still be met.
items.feedback: This is strongest because it names the process clearly and explains the goal of meeting deadlines.

items.prompt: Which sentence best links tools with effective time management?
options: Tools such as planners, calendars, reminders, and checklists help workers organise tasks, monitor progress, and stay on schedule.
Tools are not important if someone just works harder.
A good worker should rely on memory instead of using planning tools.
items.correct: Tools such as planners, calendars, reminders, and checklists help workers organise tasks, monitor progress, and stay on schedule.
items.feedback: This is strongest because it explains both the tools and why they improve deadline control.

personal-finance.title: Finance Stability Lab
personal-finance.subtitle: Track the money, protect the essentials, seek support, then upgrade the EST answer.

steps.title: Step 1: Track the Money Signal
steps.instruction: Identify the strongest budgeting or tracking move in each situation.

items.prompt: A student wants to understand why they keep running short before payday.
options: Track money in and money out so spending patterns and problem areas become visible.
Keep guessing because writing it down takes too long.
Only check the account balance when it is already low.
items.correct: Track money in and money out so spending patterns and problem areas become visible.
items.feedback: Correct. Tracking income and expenses is the first move because it shows where money is actually going.

items.prompt: Which action best shows budgeting discipline rather than wishful thinking?
options: List regular and irregular expenses, then compare them against income for the week.
Assume there will be enough money because there usually is.
Spend first and work out the totals later.
items.correct: List regular and irregular expenses, then compare them against income for the week.
items.feedback: Correct. Budgets work when both predictable and less predictable costs are visible.

items.prompt: A worker's hours change from week to week. What is the strongest money-management move?
options: Review income records regularly so the budget can adjust when earnings rise or fall.
Use the highest earning week as the budget for every week.
Ignore the variation and hope it evens out.
items.correct: Review income records regularly so the budget can adjust when earnings rise or fall.
items.feedback: Correct. Tracking helps people respond quickly when income becomes irregular.

steps.title: Step 2: Protect the Essentials
steps.instruction: Choose the response that best protects financial stability when pressure hits.

items.prompt: Sami's hours drop, creating a weekly deficit. What should be reviewed first?
options: Essential costs such as transport, food, rent, bills, and other obligations that must still be covered.
Only entertainment spending because it is the most fun to keep.
Nothing yet, because changing the budget too early is overreacting.
items.correct: Essential costs such as transport, food, rent, bills, and other obligations that must still be covered.
items.feedback: Correct. Strong budgeting starts by protecting essentials before optional spending.

items.prompt: A person needs to cut spending after an unexpected bill. Which move is strongest?
options: Reduce non-essential spending first and keep reviewing the budget until the situation stabilises.
Ignore the bill and keep all normal spending the same.
Cancel all food and transport costs immediately.
items.correct: Reduce non-essential spending first and keep reviewing the budget until the situation stabilises.
items.feedback: Correct. The strongest move is to protect essentials and reduce optional costs first.

items.prompt: Why is maintaining an emergency fund useful?
options: It provides a buffer when income drops or unexpected expenses appear.
It removes the need to budget at all.
It should only be used for impulse purchases.
items.correct: It provides a buffer when income drops or unexpected expenses appear.
items.feedback: Correct. Emergency savings improve preparedness when circumstances change unexpectedly.

steps.title: Step 3: Seek the Right Support
steps.instruction: Decide which assistance source or response is most reliable and effective.

items.prompt: A young worker is confused about managing debt and urgent bills. What is the strongest first support option?
options: Use a trusted service such as MoneySmart, a financial counsellor, or the National Debt Helpline.
Follow a random influencer who promises instant money hacks.
Avoid seeking advice because support means failure.
items.correct: Use a trusted service such as MoneySmart, a financial counsellor, or the National Debt Helpline.
items.feedback: Correct. Assistance should come from reliable, trustworthy sources rather than pressure or guesswork.

items.prompt: A student's shifts fall and they may miss payments. Which action best shows effective support-seeking?
options: Contact the bank, employer, or relevant support service early to discuss options and changed circumstances.
Wait until everything becomes unmanageable before saying anything.
Hide the problem and stop checking messages.
items.correct: Contact the bank, employer, or relevant support service early to discuss options and changed circumstances.
items.feedback: Correct. Early communication often creates more options, such as payment plans or extra shifts.

items.prompt: Why is reliable advice important in financial situations?
options: Reliable advice helps people avoid scams, bad decisions, and unnecessary financial risk.
Any advice is fine if it sounds confident.
Good financial choices should always be made quickly without checking.
items.correct: Reliable advice helps people avoid scams, bad decisions, and unnecessary financial risk.
items.feedback: Correct. Trusted advice improves confidence and reduces the chance of costly mistakes.

steps.title: Step 4: Upgrade the Answer
steps.instruction: Choose the sentence that sounds most like a stronger EST answer.

items.prompt: Which sentence best explains why budgeting matters?
options: Budgeting helps a person balance income and expenses, identify unnecessary spending, and prepare for future costs or financial change.
Budgeting is just writing numbers down with no real purpose.
Budgeting only matters if someone earns a very high income.
items.correct: Budgeting helps a person balance income and expenses, identify unnecessary spending, and prepare for future costs or financial change.
items.feedback: This is strongest because it explains both the process and why it supports financial control.

items.prompt: Which sentence best explains how people should respond to unexpected financial events?
options: Unexpected financial events should be managed by reviewing the budget, prioritising essentials, seeking reliable assistance, and adjusting plans where needed.
Unexpected events mean planning is pointless.
The best response is to keep spending the same and hope the issue disappears.
items.correct: Unexpected financial events should be managed by reviewing the budget, prioritising essentials, seeking reliable assistance, and adjusting plans where needed.
items.feedback: This is strongest because it combines practical action with adaptability and support-seeking.

job-application.title: Application Forge
job-application.subtitle: Build stronger job application responses by choosing the move that earns more marks.

rounds.title: Cover letter purpose
rounds.prompt: You are applying for a retail role. What makes the cover letter strongest?
options: Mention the specific job advertisement, explain why you are applying, and highlight your suitability briefly.
Write a generic note that never names the role because it can be reused anywhere.
Repeat the resume word for word without linking it to the employer.
rounds.correct: Mention the specific job advertisement, explain why you are applying, and highlight your suitability briefly.
rounds.feedback: Strong response. A cover letter should target the specific role and make a positive first impression.

rounds.title: Selection criteria
rounds.prompt: You need to respond to teamwork selection criteria in an interview. What should you do?
options: Use STAR so the employer can see the situation, task, action, and result clearly.
Give a vague answer because details might sound like bragging.
Talk only about what the team did and leave out your own action.
rounds.correct: Use STAR so the employer can see the situation, task, action, and result clearly.
rounds.feedback: Strong response. STAR helps structure evidence so the response is clear and relevant.

rounds.title: Interview communication
rounds.prompt: The interviewer asks a question you did not fully understand. What is the best move?
options: Ask politely for clarification so you can answer the actual question well.
Guess what they meant and answer quickly so you sound confident.
Stay silent and hope they move to the next question.
rounds.correct: Ask politely for clarification so you can answer the actual question well.
rounds.feedback: Strong response. Clarifying shows active listening and improves answer accuracy.

communication.title: Communication Radar
communication.subtitle: Sort the behaviours into effective communication or weak communication.

cards.text: A worker listens fully, nods, and asks, 'Do you mean these arranged by size?'
cards.feedback: This shows active listening because the worker checks understanding before acting.

cards.text: A team member checks their phone while a customer explains the issue.
cards.feedback: This weakens communication because it suggests poor listening and low respect.

cards.text: An applicant uses calm tone, eye contact, and clear language in an interview.
cards.feedback: This combines verbal and non-verbal communication to build rapport and clarity.

cards.text: A worker uses slang and rushed explanations with a confused customer.
cards.feedback: Messages should suit the audience. Unclear language creates misunderstandings.

future-of-work.title: Future Signals Scanner
future-of-work.subtitle: Spot the shift, read the industry signal, use the data, then upgrade the EST answer.

steps.title: Step 1: Spot the Shift
steps.instruction: Choose the statement that best shows how megatrends change work over time.

items.prompt: Which statement best explains the impact of megatrends on jobs?
options: Megatrends can create emerging jobs and change existing ones, so workers may need new skills.
Megatrends only affect jobs in one industry at a time.
Megatrends mean all existing jobs disappear completely.
items.correct: Megatrends can create emerging jobs and change existing ones, so workers may need new skills.
items.feedback: Strong call. The future of work is usually about jobs changing, evolving, or emerging rather than simply disappearing.

items.prompt: Which type of work is most likely to be reduced or reshaped by automation and more efficient processes?
options: Routine or repetitive tasks
Every specialised role equally
Only outdoor work
items.correct: Routine or repetitive tasks
items.feedback: Correct. Lower-skill, repetitive, or routine tasks are often the most exposed to automation.

items.prompt: A job now uses online systems, digital communication, and new technology tools. What does this show?
options: The role is evolving as work changes
The industry no longer matters
Technology has no impact on work
items.correct: The role is evolving as work changes
items.feedback: Strong move. Many roles stay, but their tasks, tools, and expectations evolve.

steps.title: Step 2: Read the Industry Signal
steps.instruction: Choose the label that best fits each future-of-work idea.

items.prompt: An industry is expanding over time and creating increased demand for workers.
options: Growth industry
Green industry
Labour market information
items.correct: Growth industry
items.feedback: A growth industry expands over time and can create more employment opportunities.

items.prompt: An industry is new, developing, or becoming more important because of technology or changing needs.
options: Emerging industry
Declining job
Participation rate
items.correct: Emerging industry
items.feedback: Emerging industries are new or developing and often linked to megatrends.

items.prompt: An industry reduces environmental harm or supports cleaner and more sustainable ways of working.
options: Green industry
Growth industry
Employment level
items.correct: Green industry
items.feedback: Green industries support sustainability and can include both new and changing roles.

items.prompt: Data about wages, job vacancies, industry growth, and employment trends is called:
options: Labour market information
Customer feedback
Selection criteria
items.correct: Labour market information
items.feedback: Labour market information helps people make career decisions using evidence about jobs and industries.

steps.title: Step 3: Use the Data
steps.instruction: Choose the strongest interpretation of the labour market evidence.

items.prompt: Employment data shows Health Care and Social Assistance grew strongly, while Manufacturing declined. What is the best interpretation?
options: Growth is uneven across industries, so some areas create more opportunities than others.
Every industry is growing at the same rate.
Industry data is useless for career decisions.
items.correct: Growth is uneven across industries, so some areas create more opportunities than others.
items.feedback: This is the strongest interpretation because labour market growth is not spread evenly.

items.prompt: National data shows an industry is growing, but there are few local opportunities where you live. What is the strongest conclusion?
options: Macro data still needs to be interpreted in relation to local opportunities and your own situation.
National growth guarantees a job for everyone locally.
Location never affects employment opportunities.
items.correct: Macro data still needs to be interpreted in relation to local opportunities and your own situation.
items.feedback: Strong move. Labour market information is useful, but it has to be interpreted at the micro level too.

items.prompt: Projected employment growth is strongest in higher-skill occupations. What does that suggest?
options: Training, qualifications, and adaptable skills may be increasingly important for future opportunities.
Skill level has no effect on future job growth.
Only entry-level roles will expand.
items.correct: Training, qualifications, and adaptable skills may be increasingly important for future opportunities.
items.feedback: Correct. The strongest growth is often linked to roles requiring post-secondary skills or specialised knowledge.

steps.title: Step 4: Upgrade the Answer
steps.instruction: Choose the sentence that sounds most like a stronger EST answer.

items.prompt: Which sentence best explains how megatrends influence the future of work?
options: Megatrends influence the future of work by changing industries, creating emerging jobs, and reshaping the skills workers need over time.
Megatrends are just interesting ideas with no effect on careers.
Megatrends only matter to government, not workers.
items.correct: Megatrends influence the future of work by changing industries, creating emerging jobs, and reshaping the skills workers need over time.
items.feedback: This is strongest because it explains both the change and its impact on workers.

items.prompt: Which sentence best explains why labour market information matters?
options: Labour market information helps people compare growth areas, wages, job demand, and skill needs so they can make better career decisions.
Labour market information is only useful after someone already has a permanent job.
Labour market information mainly tells you what subjects to like.
items.correct: Labour market information helps people compare growth areas, wages, job demand, and skill needs so they can make better career decisions.
items.feedback: This is strongest because it links the evidence directly to career planning.
